**Kanso Protocol `0.1.0`** — first public alpha.

An open source Angular design system built around architectural consistency: every value is a token, every primitive ships with light + dark themes, and the same W3C DTCG tokens drive both Figma and code.

**Live Storybook:** https://gregnblack.github.io/kanso-protocol/

## What's in the box

- **36 components** — form controls (inputs, selects, combobox, slider, date/timepickers, etc.), overlays (dialog, drawer, popover, menu, tooltip, toast), data display (table, tree, badge, avatar, progress), and the usual suspects (button, accordion, tabs, breadcrumbs, pagination…).
- **20 layout patterns** — `app-shell`, `page-header`, `sidebar`, `stack`, `grid`, `filter-bar`, `search-bar`, `notification-center`, `settings-panel`, and more.
- **Foundations** — W3C DTCG tokens (color, spacing, radius, motion, type), light + dark themes, CSS-first composition so consumers can re-skin any component without forking.
- **Reactive Forms integration** — every form control implements `ControlValueAccessor`. `form-field` auto-detects the attached `NgControl` and renders validation messages via the `KP_VALIDATION_MESSAGES` DI token.
- **Rich text editor** (built on TipTap) with `ControlValueAccessor`, toolbar, inline link editor, and an `(imageUpload)` hook for host-app storage.
- **AI-native via MCP.** [`@kanso-protocol/mcp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@kanso-protocol/mcp) is a [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) server that exposes the live catalog (components, patterns, tokens) to Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP-aware client. Wire it via `npx @kanso-protocol/mcp` and your assistant authors Kanso UI from the actual API surface.
- **352 unit tests** across 38 spec files cover every shipped component — [see the live coverage page](https://gregnblack.github.io/kanso-protocol/?path=/docs/foundations-test-coverage--docs).

## Install

```sh
npm install @kanso-protocol/button @kanso-protocol/input @kanso-protocol/form-field
# or any of the other 53 packages — every component and pattern is its own npm package
```

Every component is standalone and tree-shakeable. You only pay for what you import.

## ⚠ Status: alpha

The public API is intentionally unstable until `1.0`. Any minor bump (`0.x.y`) may include breaking changes — **pin exact versions in production** and read the [`CHANGELOG`](https://github.com/GregNBlack/kanso-protocol/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) before upgrading.

### Known limitations for `0.1.0`

- No SSR validation yet (components are tested in browser / jsdom only).
- Manual a11y audit pending (sensible ARIA + keyboard handling ship by default; formal screen reader / focus-trap pass lands on `0.2.x`).
- LTR only — RTL support is on the roadmap.
- Onest font + Tabler icons are referenced via the host app's stack; no CDN vendoring inside packages yet.
